questions.explanation: Frijoles is plural, so use me gustan. Me gusta is for singular nouns.

questions.prompt: How do you ask for the check in Mexico?
questions.explanation: La cuenta, por favor = The bill, please. Waiters in Mexico don't bring it automatically.

questions.prompt: What does 'rico' mean when describing food?
questions.explanation: Rico/a = delicious when talking about food. ¡Está muy rico! = It's very delicious!

questions.prompt: How do you say 'I want a water, please'?
options: Me gusta un agua, por favor.
questions.explanation: Quiero = I want. Quiero un agua, por favor is the standard way to order.

options: What you say when someone sneezes
What you say to wish someone an enjoyable meal
A greeting used only at breakfast
questions.explanation: ¡Buen provecho! = Enjoy your meal! Said when someone starts eating — equivalent to 'bon appétit'.

questions.prompt: Which drink is typically called 'refresco' in Mexico?
questions.explanation: Refresco = soda or soft drink in Mexican Spanish. Coca-Cola and other sodas are all refrescos.

questions.prompt: How do you say 'Do you like coffee?' informally?
questions.explanation: ¿Te gusta...? = Do you like...? (informal). Le gusta is formal/third person.

questions.prompt: What UNESCO recognition does Mexican cuisine have?
questions.explanation: Mexican cuisine is recognized by UNESCO as an Intangible Cultural Heritage — one of only a few national cuisines with this distinction.
